de.uka.ilkd.key.symbolic_execution.strategy.breakpoint

Class AbstractBreakpoint

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.symbolic_execution.strategy.breakpoint.AbstractBreakpoint
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    IBreakpoint
    Direct Known Subclasses:
    AbstractHitCountBreakpoint

    public abstract class AbstractBreakpoint
extends Object
implements IBreakpoint
    Provides the basic implementation of an IBreakpoint.
    Author:
    Martin Hentschel

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field and Description
      private boolean enabled
      The flag if the Breakpoint is enabled.
      private Proof proof
      The proof this stop condition is associated with.

      boolean isEnabled()
      Checks if the Breakpoint is enabled.
      void setEnabled(boolean enabled)
      Sets the new enabled value.
      void updateState(int maxApplications, long timeout, Proof proof, IGoalChooser goalChooser, long startTime, int countApplied, Goal goal)
      This method is called from ApplyStrategy.IStopCondition.isGoalAllowed(int, long, Proof, IGoalChooser, long, int, Goal) and can be used to update the state of the IBreakpoint.

    • Constructor Detail

      • AbstractBreakpoint

        public AbstractBreakpoint(Proof proof,
                  boolean enabled)
        Constructor.
        Parameters:
        proof - The Proof in which this IBreakpoint is used.
        enabled - The enabled state.

    • Method Detail

      • updateState

        public void updateState(int maxApplications,
               long timeout,
               Proof proof,
               IGoalChooser goalChooser,
               long startTime,
               int countApplied,
               Goal goal)
        This method is called from ApplyStrategy.IStopCondition.isGoalAllowed(int, long, Proof, IGoalChooser, long, int, Goal) and can be used to update the state of the IBreakpoint.
        Specified by:
        updateState in interface IBreakpoint
        Parameters:
        maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply. Can be different to StrategySettings.getMaxSteps() in side proofs.
        timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled. Can be different to StrategySettings.getTimeout() in side proofs.
        proof - The current Proof.
        goalChooser - The current IGoalChooser.
        startTime - The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via System.currentTimeMillis()
        countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
        goal - The current Goal on which the next rule will be applied.

      • isEnabled

        public boolean isEnabled()
        Checks if the Breakpoint is enabled.
        Specified by:
        isEnabled in interface IBreakpoint
        Returns:
        true if Breakpoint is enabled

      • setEnabled

        public void setEnabled(boolean enabled)
        Sets the new enabled value.
        Parameters:
        enabled - the new value

      • getProof

        public Proof getProof()
        Returns:
        the proof
